word_list.word: a contented mind is a perpetual feast
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ kəntˈɛntᵻd mˈaɪnd ɪz ɐ pɚpˈɛtʃuːəl fˈiːst

senses.definition: used as a reminder that true happiness and contentment come from within, and that a person who is satisfied with what they have will always feel fulfilled and satisfied

examples.x: The wealthy businessman realized too late that a contented mind is a perpetual feast, and that money couldn't buy him true happiness.

word_list.word: humble hearts (often|tend to|) have humble desires
word_list.pronunciation: hˈʌmbəl hˈɑːɹts ˈɔfən tˈɛnd tʊ hæv hˈʌmbəl dɪzˈaɪɚz

senses.definition: used to suggest that modest and unpretentious people tend to have more realistic expectations and are less likely to be disappointed or frustrated by unattainable goals or desires

examples.x: While his friends sought fame and glory, the artist was most fulfilled when bringing joy to others through his craft; humble hearts have humble desires.

word_list.word: bird in the hand is worth two in the bush
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɜːd ɪnðə hˈænd ɪz wˈɜːθ tˈuː ɪnðə bˈʊʃ

senses.definition: used to imply that it is better to hold onto something you already have for sure, rather than risking it for the possibility of getting more in the future

examples.x: I know you want a better job, but remember, a bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
Don't sell your car just yet; a bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
The old saying "a bird in the hand is worth two in the bush" kept her from quitting her stable job.
Sometimes, sticking with what you know is the safest bet—after all, a bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
She was tempted to leave for a new opportunity, but realized a bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.

word_list.word: a happy heart is better than a full purse
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ hˈæpi hˈɑːɹt ɪz bˈɛɾɚ ðˌænə fˈʊl pˈɜːs

senses.definition: used to emphasize that true happiness and contentment come from within, and that material wealth is not a guarantee of happiness or fulfillment

word_list.word: better an egg in peace than an ox in war
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚɹ ɐn ˈɛɡ ɪn pˈiːs ðˌænən ˈɑːks ɪn wˈɔːɹ

senses.definition: used to emphasize that having a peaceful and contented life is more important than having wealth or material possessions that come at the cost of constant conflict or stress

word_list.word: better an egg today than a hen tomorrow
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚɹ ɐn ˈɛɡ tədˈeɪ ðˌænə hˈɛn təmˈɔːɹoʊ

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of valuing what one has in the present moment instead of waiting for something better in the future, which may not materialize

examples.x: I was tempted to hold out for a higher salary, but then I remembered the old saying 'better an egg today than a hen tomorrow.'

word_list.word: better half an egg than an empty shell
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚ hˈæf ɐn ˈɛɡ ðˌænən ˈɛmpti ʃˈɛl

senses.definition: used as a reminder to be grateful for what one has and to appreciate the value of even small possessions or accomplishments

examples.x: Although the bonus was smaller than she expected, she reminded herself that 'better half an egg than an empty shell' and was grateful for the extra money.

senses.definition: used to imply that one should appreciate and value the small things in life, as even small pleasures or accomplishments can bring joy and satisfaction

examples.x: The child's laughter reminded me that little fish are sweet, and that sometimes the simplest things can bring the greatest joy.

word_list.word: when all fruit fails, welcome haws
word_list.pronunciation: wˌɛn ˈɔːl fɹˈuːt fˈeɪlz wˈɛlkʌm hˈɔːz

senses.definition: used to suggest that when one's first choice or preferred option is not available, one should be flexible and willing to accept something else that may be less desirable but still of value

word_list.word: if you cannot live longer, live deeper
word_list.pronunciation: ɪf juː kænˈɑːt lˈɪv lˈɑːŋɡɚ lˈaɪv dˈiːpɚ

senses.definition: used to emphasize that pursuing a purposeful and fulfilling life is more valuable than simply living a long time

word_list.word: it is better to travel hopefully than to arrive
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz bˈɛɾɚ tə tɹˈævəl hˈoʊpfəli ðɐn tʊ ɐɹˈaɪv

senses.definition: used to imply that the experience of working towards a goal can be fulfilling in itself, and that the journey is often more valuable than the destination

examples.x: The athlete found joy in the training and the process of improving, rather than just winning the competition, living by the philosophy that it is better to travel hopefully than to arrive.

word_list.word: being happy is better than being king
word_list.pronunciation: bˌiːɪŋ hˈæpi ɪz bˈɛɾɚ ðɐn bˌiːɪŋ kˈɪŋ

senses.definition: used to emphasize that personal happiness and contentment are more important than power or status, as material possessions do not necessarily lead to happiness

examples.x: The athlete retired from professional sports to spend more time with their family, recognizing that being happy is better than being king.

word_list.word: better a lean peace than a fat victory
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚɹ ɐ lˈiːn pˈiːs ðˌænə fˈæt vˈɪktɚɹi

senses.definition: used to imply that it is better to achieve a peaceful resolution, even if it means making some compromises, rather than achieving a victory that comes at a high cost

examples.x: The business partners chose to compromise on certain terms to ensure a mutually beneficial agreement, acknowledging that better a lean peace than a fat victory.

word_list.word: it is better to be happy than wise
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz bˈɛɾɚ təbi hˈæpi ðɐn wˈaɪz

senses.definition: used to imply that personal happiness and fulfillment are more important than knowledge or academic achievement

examples.x: The scientist chose a career path that allowed for a healthy work-life balance, believing that it is better to be happy than wise.
